mnemonics: FLAT: Fewer Locks And Two-phase commit. NESTED: Numerous Executions Sequentially Transacted, Encapsulated, & Delegated.
keyPoints: A flat transaction is a single, atomic unit of work with a single BEGIN and COMMIT/ABORT.
A nested transaction is a hierarchy of subtransactions where a parent can have multiple children; only the top-level transaction can commit.
Nested transactions allow for partial rollbacks (aborting a subtransaction without aborting the entire parent transaction), which improves modularity and concurrency.
commonMistakes: Assuming a subtransaction in a nested model can independently commit its results to the permanent database (only the root transaction can).
Confusing a nested transaction with a distributed transaction (a distributed transaction can be flat or nested).
Believing that aborting a nested subtransaction always forces the entire parent transaction to abort (it only forces an abort if the parent chooses to handle it that way).
